headline: Action Chain System Design
orientation: This document is a technical design specification for the Action Chain system, written as a Markdown file (action-chain-system.md) and dated implicitly by its versioning (schema_version 1). It describes a Rust (Tauri) backend that replaces hard‑coded build pipelines with data‑driven YAML chain definitions, outlines the runtime architecture, schema, execution model, error handling, model configuration, and future extensions (Vine, Delta, Meta, Wire sync, frontend). The main purpose is to enable flexible, resumable, observable pipelines for conversation, code, and document processing while supporting variant chains and agent‑driven authoring.

topics.current: The Action Chain system replaces ~8,000 lines of duplicated Rust build pipelines (build_conversation, build_code, build_docs) with YAML‑defined execution chains. It provides pipelines as data, per‑step model selection, resume support, variant chains, per‑step observability (timing, token counts, cost logged to pyramid_cost_log), and a path toward agent‑driven chain authoring. Scope v1 covers conversation, code, and document pipelines; Vine, delta, and meta chains are deferred to wave 2.
decisions: Replace hard‑coded Rust pipelines with data‑driven YAML chains
Limit v1 to conversation, code, and document pipelines
topics.name: Overview & Goals

topics.current: Six Rust modules in src‑tauri/src/pyramid/ implement the chain runtime: chain_engine.rs (schema & validation), chain_resolve.rs (ref and template resolution), chain_dispatch.rs (LLM/mechanical dispatch & node construction), chain_executor.rs (main loop with modes and resume), chain_loader.rs (YAML loading & prompt refs), chain_registry.rs (SQLite assignment table). Execution proceeds via run_chain_build(slug) → chain_registry → chain_loader → chain_executor::execute_chain, building a ChainContext and StepContext, spawning a writer drain task, iterating steps, handling when‑conditions, modes (mechanical, recursive_pair, pair_adjacent, forEach, single), resume checks, LLM/mechanical dispatch, node persistence, and progress reporting, finally returning apex_node_id and failure_count.
decisions: Implement chain runtime as six Rust modules
Use SQLite for chain‑to‑slug mapping and assignment
Support async writer drain for SQLite writes
topics.name: Architecture & Execution Flow

topics.current: The YAML schema (schema_version 1) defines top‑level fields (id, name, description, content_type, version, author, defaults, steps, post_build). ChainDefaults include model_tier, model, temperature, on_error. ChainStep includes name, primitive (28 predefined + custom), instruction (prompt file), mechanical flag, rust_function, input, output_schema, model overrides, temperature, sequential, accumulate, for_each, pair_adjacent, recursive_pair, batch_threshold, merge_instruction, when, on_error, save_as, node_id_pattern, depth. Validation enforces required fields, unique step names, primitive list, mechanical Lrust_function requirement, LLM instruction requirement, on_error syntax, mutual exclusivity of pair flags, and sequential‑for_each dependency. Variable resolution works via $variable.path in YAML inputs (built‑in scalars, forEach/recursive_pair loop variables, step output references) and {{variable}} in Markdown prompts. Undefined required references cause runtime errors.
decisions: Adopt a single schema version (1) for all chains
Validate primitives against a fixed list of 28 categories
topics.name: Chain Definition Schema & Variable Resolution

topics.current: Error strategies (abort, skip, retry(N), carry_left/up) are defined per step or via defaults. Retry uses exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s…). LLM steps have an automatic JSON‑parse retry at temperature 0.1 before invoking the step‑level strategy. Model configuration uses a tier system (low, mid, high, max) mapping to OpenRouter slugs defined in LlmConfig. Precedence: step model > defaults model > step model_tier > defaults model_tier. Temperature defaults to 0.3 and can be overridden per step.
decisions: Implement five error handling strategies with defined precedence
Provide automatic JSON‑parse retry for LLM calls
Map model tiers to configurable OpenRouter slugs
topics.name: Error Handling & Model Configuration

topics.current: Wave 2 introduces Vine (meta‑pyramid aggregating existing pyramids), Delta (incremental rebuild on new chunks), and Meta (metadata overlay nodes). Wave 3 adds Wire sync for publishing/downloading chain definitions across agents, and a frontend Chain Manager/Editor with drag‑to‑reorder UI, schema import/export, and agent handoff features. Variant chains allow inline prompt editing without affecting shared defaults.
decisions: Defer Vine, Delta, Meta to Wave 2
Enable Wire network synchronization for chain distribution
Provide UI for chain management and editing in Phase 6‑8
topics.name: Future Roadmap
